_doc: TEMPORARY local versification assignments (per aligned iso) — a STOPGAP until bcv-commons/bibles publishes the per-text `versification` field, at which point the aligner should SOURCE this from there (the curated overlay: meta/texts/<iso>/<textId>.json → `versification`) and this file goes away. Values use the bibles schema enum: protestant | hebrew | septuagint | vulgate | orthodox | catholic | other. Only NON-protestant entries actually matter for remapping (protestant = KJV-standard = identity with our source numbering); the protestant rows are recorded for completeness / future-proofing.
_rus_note: rus = SEPTUAGINT (LXX Psalm renumbering) — CORRECTED 2026-07-13. The earlier `hebrew` guess was WRONG: fingerprinting our INGESTED data/usj-rus verse structure against the 7 CDN /_vrs/ schemes shows rus_syn matches rso (Russian Orthodox) 99.7% and lxx Psalms 149/150, but hebrew/org only 17/150 Psalms (133 mismatched). CDN index labels helloao:rus_syn=`vul`, but the delivered text matches rso/lxx Psalms better than vul (mismatch 1 vs 7) — so the empirical structure, not the label, is ground truth. We hold no rso/vul table (CDN .vrs are structure-only; bcv-commons/versification HF is gone), but lxx.tsv reproduces the rus Psalter at 149/150 → use `septuagint`. The old 'lxx made rus worse 44→42%' was scored vs the DEFECTIVE Clear rus gold (see internal-docs/clear-rus-defect.md), not the text. Robust method for any lang: fingerprint ingested USJ vs CDN schemes, map to best available table.
_fra_swe_note: fra/swe/swk = HEBREW (org Psalm superscription) — CORRECTED 2026-07-13. Fingerprinting data/usj-{fra-lsg,swe,swk} vs CDN /_vrs/ schemes: all match orgw (fra 100%, swe 96%, swk 97%), org PSA-mismatch=0, but eng/identity mismatches 62 superscripted Psalms. CDN index labels these orgw; no orgw table exists (CDN .vrs structure-only), but hebrew.tsv (org) reproduces their Psalter exactly (PSA-mismatch=0); org-vs-orgw residual is non-Psalm single-verse boundaries only. NT unaffected (hebrew.tsv has no NT diffs) so gold scores hold.
